finding.contract_claim: Parameter `plumbing_result` is declared in the signature of build_scorecard(config, analysis_results, junk_results=None, dead_code_results=None, plumbing_result=None) (signature at lines 106-113) as an accepted input that callers may pass.
finding.observed_behavior: No call site ever passes `plumbing_result`: the only caller, run_audit in src/osoji/audit.py, invokes build_scorecard with config, analysis_results and junk_results only, so the parameter always takes its default None and the branches gated on it never execute.

finding.triage_reasoning: Reality: Same analysis as claim 8. The `plumbing_result` parameter is explicitly labeled `# Backward compat:` and the only known caller (`run_audit` in `audit.py`) never passes it, making the `plumbing_result is not None` branch at scorecard.py:239 permanently dead. The parameter always takes its default `None`. The evidence sweep covered 2 files with 8 matches all within scorecard.py itself. Significance: The dead branch adds complexity. Actionability: Remove or formally deprecate.
finding.suggested_fix: If backward compatibility is no longer required, remove the `plumbing_result` parameter and the guarded block at scorecard.py:237-246. Otherwise, document the deprecation timeline and emit a DeprecationWarning when a non-None value is passed.
